metadata.title: The Illusion of Control (Dark Psychology)
metadata.tags: Psychology, Manipulation, Persuasion, Control, Sigma, ShadowLaws, Shorts

script_segments.text: People don't hate doing work; they hate being told to do it. Resistance is born from a lack of choice.
script_segments.visual_keywords: silhouette behind bars, man pushing wall

script_segments.text: To eliminate resistance, use the 'False Choice.' Never give a single command. Always give two options that both serve your goal.
script_segments.visual_keywords: chess hand move, fork in road

script_segments.text: Instead of 'Do this task,' say: 'Do you want to finish this now, or would you prefer to have it done by tomorrow morning?'
Their brain narrows its focus to the choice itself. By choosing 'how' to do it, they subconsciously commit to 'doing' it.
You are giving them the steering wheel, but you are the one who drew the map.
Give them the choice. Keep the control. Because...
